input.counterSource: CSSWG resolution Jan 31 2025 (via CSS-Tricks "Masonry Layout is Now grid-lanes"); W3C CSS WG Blog March 28 2025 (https://www.w3.org/blog/CSS/2025/03/28/css-grid-1-2-3-updated/); MDN Web Docs "Masonry layout" (display: grid-lanes / inline-grid-lanes); Chrome for Developers "An alternative proposal for CSS masonry"
input.evidence: The WebKit blog post accurately describes one of two competing proposals ("Just Use Grid" via grid-template-rows: masonry), and the supporting quote is correctly cited. However, the claim is refuted on multiple grounds: (1) The source is Apple's advocacy post for their preferred approach in an active CSSWG debate, not a specification statement. (2) The CSSWG resolved in January 2025 to reject the "just use grid" approach in favor of a new display type. The final syntax is display: grid-lanes (and inline-grid-lanes), which IS a new display value — not merely one new value added to grid-template-rows/columns. (3) The Firefox-only grid-template-rows: masonry syntax was explicitly dropped by CSSWG. Safari 26.4+ now implements display: grid-lanes. (4) Smashing Magazine (May 2025), CSS-Tricks, and the W3C CSS WG blog (March 2025) all confirm the grid-lanes resolution, directly contradicting the claim's framing. The claim accurately quotes the source but presents a rejected proposal as if it describes how masonry can actually be implemented, which as of 2025 is false.
